# The Witnesses — Shared Log

*session-runner appends a one-paragraph summary after each session. Append only; do not edit prior entries.*

**S7 — The Keystone (2026-04-21) — CAMPAIGN COMPLETE:** Magistra Doran was waiting at the exterior when the party returned from the Crypt. She came herself. They brought her inside. Calla stood in the center of the nave and spoke Velantha's true account to all assembled factions: the congregation (all sections together for the first time), Aldric Voss with his new ceramic holder, Velantha present, Doran with her commission document. Maret performed her three breaths privately before the account, facing the wall, for herself. The line that fired Doran's decision came quietly: "She never asked for a cathedral. She asked for medicine." Doran was quiet for a long time. Then: "The town I know will not like this. I am doing it anyway." She signed the commission for the second cathedral and ceded this one to the congregation. The congregation chiseled Velantha's name into the keystone's blank face. Dren set the stone (Strength 19 — it dropped cleanly). The arch held. Maret held the journal as the keystone was set; it rewrote: "They set the stone with my name in it. That is what I would have asked for, if anyone had thought to ask. I am glad." Renn asked Dren what he was going to do. Dren said he would find Aldis's family. He kept the insignia. The building is what it actually is. 1,500 XP each. Route D. CAMPAIGN COMPLETE.

**S6 — The Crypt (2026-04-21):** The Crypt's cold cost Renn 3 HP on entry. Velantha was present in the finished room the congregation built over thirty years. She told her account; confirmed Aldric's version with one correction ("he couldn't bring himself to write that I was glad"); described the overextension. When she said "I reached past what my body could sustain for people the Orders had decided were not worth it," Renn said aloud: "She used something that wasn't prayer. I know what that costs." Velantha asked: "What was yours?" He told the party: an Istaran wayhouse, an unnamed ancient thing, a sister he could not save with the power he received. Three sentences. Maret read the journal's final entry (passive 17 auto-detected the rewrite): "I should have told them it was sorcery. I should have told them gully dwarves are worth saving. I didn't, because I thought a miracle would be more useful than the truth. I was wrong about that. I was right about everything else." Maret performed three breaths after reading it. Dren found the inscription — "SHE CAME WHEN WE ASKED" — and said it aloud once, to himself. Velantha asked what the party would do; they didn't know yet; Calla asked what Velantha would want; Velantha said: "I want the gully dwarves to have somewhere that is theirs. They deserve the building." She gave Calla the journal. Calla said: "I'll try." 1,100 XP each. Route D.

**S5 — The Sacristy (2026-04-21):** Renn Ashkeld, whose power comes from an unauthorized pact, entered Aldric Voss's room and recognized immediately what thirty years of unauthorized record-keeping looks like. The party cleared the east passage and found Aldric mid-sentence. Calla tried Thunderwave on the dispensation letter; the letter was unchanged; the ceramic holder shattered; Aldric made a note to make a new one. Hint 3 delivered: Aldric gave Velantha the prohibited working knowingly and never corrected the false miracle narrative. Maret answered his question ("What do you practice, and who gave you permission?") — after answering, she picked up the letter and felt it warm for the first time. Renn told Aldric his parallel via Awakened Mind; the party didn't hear. Arc-completion fired: "I regret that I let them call it a miracle. She would have hated that. She never asked for a cathedral. She asked for medicine." Aldric gave them his copy and pointed them toward the Crypt. 900 XP each. Route D.

**S4 — The Transept Witness (2026-04-21):** After a short rest (Calla healed to full), the Witnesses entered the Transept and found the elder Morren waiting in blue-green light — the colour the congregation had chosen for reasons that were right for them and wrong for Mishakal. Dren spotted the unstable floor before anyone stepped on it. The ritual focus hummed once for Renn. Morren identified the mark as Aldric Voss's. He told his account in seven stages; Calla listened without asking a single question; Morren said "She did not call it a miracle. She called it the right thing to do" — the line the congregation does not have. Hint 2 delivered (the working was not prayer; it went through both temperatures quickly). Hint 3 planted (Aldric is in the Sacristy; he gave her the working; he has been copying the account for twenty years). Renn smelled old ink from the Sacristy passage before being told. 700 XP each. Route D.

**S3 — The Bell Tower (2026-04-21):** The Witnesses entered the Bell Tower and found the congregation's aggrieved side — thirty-two years of held resentment practiced in the same forms as the nave, but tighter. Maret flanked silently through the tally alcove; Dren and Renn entered front. Calla misread Sharr's opening statement as hostile (Insight fumble); combat began. One aggrieved member hit Calla (3 damage — first party damage of the campaign). Sharr's Eldritch Blast reduced Sharr to 3 HP in round 1; morale broke. In the stillness after, Dren named Aldis and Renn named Mira in response — the bilateral arc fired. Sharr untied the promise-cord and gave it to Dren. The ritual focus fell from a congregation member's hands, warm to the touch; the mark on its base connects to whoever gave Velantha the ritual. Hint 2 missed by 1 point (recovery path active via the focus). 500 XP total. Route D.

**S2 — The Nave Congregation (2026-04-21):** The Witnesses entered the nave and found thirty years of organized practice — swept floor, pews in rows, candles in bent-nail holders, a morning rite led by Veth with a clay cup that always holds the correct amount. A rat swarm from the gatehouse followed them in; the congregation stood on the pews while Dren and Renn handled it in two rounds. The morning rite proceeded; Maret's craft-witness fired in the closing silence when she performed her three breaths in the space the rite had just occupied. In the Apse, three practitioners were performing the rite incorrectly; Calla recognized the wrong sequence and corrected it. Gorret answered Dren's honest answer to "why do you need to go up?" with "at least you say what you are" and stepped aside. Veth told Calla what he has thought for twenty-two years: "I don't know if I am doing it right. I do it anyway." He then named the Transept elder as the one who was actually there. Party at full HP, 250 XP each. Two NPC arc-completions (Veth, Gorret). Route D.

**S1 — The Gatehouse Watch (2026-04-21):** The Witnesses arrived at the Halted Spire's gatehouse under Magistra Doran's commission and found it occupied by seven gully dwarves — Velantha's congregation, led by a keeper named Mig carrying a warm leather satchel with initials V.S. scratched into the underside. Renn's Awakened Mind established communication with Mig without language; Maret noticed the warmth of the satchel before anyone touched it; Calla tried to categorize the congregation's shrine arrangements and could not. Dren destroyed a Ghoul nested in the crawlspace beneath the guardroom floor — the congregation's actual fear — in two rounds. The party then accompanied Mig to the inner door and opened it; Mig stepped through to the Nave shrine she had been trying to reach for three weeks. The inner cathedral is now accessible. Party at full HP, 0 attunements, 200 XP earned. Route D.
